name: Oliva Ascolane

ingredients.item: panko breadcrumbs or Italian bread crumbs
olive oil - plus more if needed for frying

method: Remove the pits and open 12 olives, you will be stuffing them later. Soak olives in a bowl of water with 1 t fennel for about 6 hours, I do this in the morning when I wake up if I have it for lunch. Drain and dry well. Lay on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Remove casings and break up sausage links. You can use your hands or you can use a meat grinder. In a large frying pan on medium heat, in 1 T butter and 1 T olive oil, saute onion, carrots, celery, and pepper until onion is transparent. I have omitted the salt from this recipe because the olives are salty. Add ground sausage and fry until cooked through. Add parmesan, parsley, minced garlic, and a dash of nutmeg. Mix well and turn off heat. Keep in mind you can add more salt for taste if needed. In three small bowls, mix bread crumbs and pepper in one, in another add flour, and one with beaten egg. Stuff each olive with 1 t of the sausage mixture and close it to form it's original shape. Place the olive back onto the parchment paper and continue to stuff the remaining olives. After stuffing the olives with sausage, dip each olive in flour, beaten egg, coat with breading, and set aside on parchment paper. Continue breading all 12 olives. In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil and fry the breaded stuffed olives on all sides until golden brown. Place on a paper towel to drain. Add more oil to fry if needed. Makes 1 dozen. Olé
